description_long: This object is a vintage-style lotion bottle with a trapezoidal, dark maroon glass body featuring a large embossed fleur-de-lis emblem on its front face. The bottle is topped with a tarnished brass atomizer mechanism, which includes a round, flat cap and a short, angled spout. Attached to the spout is a textured, bulbous, pink squeeze pump, likely made of fabric or rubber, used to dispense the lotion in a fine mist. The glass body shows signs of wear, with smudges and discoloration, giving it an aged, well-used appearance. Small gold dots encircle the emblem, adding a decorative touch. The overall design suggests a blend of elegance and utilitarian function, suitable for display or use in a classic urban vanity or personal care setting.
description: A vintage maroon glass lotion bottle with a brass atomizer and a pink squeeze pump, featuring an embossed fleur-de-lis emblem and decorative gold dots.
description_view_0: Front view showing the embossed fleur-de-lis emblem, gold dots, and the full atomizer assembly with the pink squeeze pump clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the bottle and the prominent pink squeeze pump, with less detail visible on the bottle's face.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the plain, maroon glass back of the bottle and the atomizer mechanism, with the squeeze pump on the left.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the bottle's thickness and the atomizer cap, with the squeeze pump partially obscured.
